campaign: HARDPAN W9 — 1 KiB LineWriter on the ratatui renderer (agent_tui.rs / tui.rs)
verdict: NULL — downgraded at GATE 0; no product change lands

console_write_cost_ab.method: Same total bytes per frame delivered as 8 x 1 KiB writes (LineWriter shape) vs 1 x 8 KiB write (BufWriter shape); 200 frames, cursor parked, real console window, [Console]::Out + Flush per frame
console_write_cost_ab.note: 8x the WriteFile calls cost 1.07x the time — console cost on this host tracks bytes, not call count.

ratatui_source_analysis.diff_only_flush: ratatui 0.29.0 Terminal::flush (terminal/terminal.rs:198-205) computes previous_buffer.diff(current_buffer) and writes ONLY changed cells; the agent TUI's steady-state diff (spinner + elapsed field + newly streamed chars) is far under 1024 bytes
ratatui_source_analysis.already_one_write: Terminal::draw always ends in show/hide-cursor via execute! (backend/crossterm.rs:211,:215,:226), which is queue-then-flush (crossterm macros.rs:114-117) — any frame whose diff is under the 1 KiB LineWriter buffer is ALREADY exactly one WriteFile today
ratatui_source_analysis.residual: Full-pane diffs (first draw, resize, overlay toggle, scroll, wrapped-line shift) are ~4-5 KB on a 120x40 console -> ~5 writes today vs ~1 buffered; a few syscalls, a few times per second, with identical byte volume delivered to the terminal either way

honest_framing.premise_check: The finding's low-level facts hold (Stdout IS a LineWriter; LineWriter::new IS 1024 bytes — std 1.95.0 io/buffered/linewriter.rs:90) but both operational claims fail: frames are diffs, not full screens, and sub-1KiB frames already coalesce to one write.
honest_framing.decision: GATE 0 downgraded W9 to a documented null; per the SIROCCO/STAMPEDE precedent for unmeasured perf claims, no BufWriter change lands and W9 is closed as measured-null.
honest_framing.reopen_if: A future renderer change starts emitting multi-KB frames every redraw (e.g. full-repaint animations), at which point the A/B above is the harness to rerun.
